restrictions o mobility; personal travel comimom from horse- back to well-established public transport systems ad privately owned cars_ Extensive networks of JumpShips ad DropShips carry people and goods between the stars though the exorbi tant costs of interstellar transport make such voyages once in-a-lifetime experience for most people.
hypereaac ualucky 25 rad chandes percapuon usualy subsida withln seconds or minutes ot a jump; though few people have been driven into catatonia by the experience Physical symptoms affect Iarger group ot people, maln nausea and dizziness. Jumping through spaca dlsturbs the inner ear, prompting physical reactions similar to those o weightlessness These symptoms can alfect evan individuals who do not sutfer conventlonal space sickness  very few cases, jump sends an individual into shock making urgent medical attention necessary: All JumpShip and DropShip crews are trained in first aid_ and medical kits are positioned at key points  throughout every vessel. Long-term rehabilitation, however; depends on the skills of professional medical staft. It is almost impossible to identify sufferers of this extreme reaction before the onset of symptoms; but once diag- nosed, the patient barred from interstellar travel:
INTEAPLANETARY AND INTERSTELLAR TRANBPORT DropShips and  JumpShips transport   Inner   Sphere and Periphery  citizens   from worild to world_ the latter using the Kearny-Fuchida drive to ferry the former across light-years of space The KF drive allowed humankind to spread far beyond Terra and ever since has helped knit the sprawling vistas of colonized space together
JumpShips and the Kearny-Fuchlda Drive From the Abridged Encyclopedia   Galactica,  Universal Press 3062 Though small refinements have been made t0 the KF drive since the first starship "jump' in 2107_ the underlying princi ples remain true to the theories put forward by Kearny ad Fuchida in 2018 In the simplest terms_ ship with KF drive can instantly travel to another point up to thirty light-years away: Of course, severa conditions limit this form of travel. For example , the drive often accounts for most of JumpShip" mass, which Iimits the amount cargo and smaller ships can carry: Because of this; JumpShips do not have interplanetary drives Instead_ they rely cargo ships equipped with interplanetary drives, known as DropShips Most JumpShips can carry one or more DropShips, JumpShip can only  Jump and  from predetermined points in space_ known as jump points_ The standard points are ocated above' and below' the plane of the star system; directly to the north and south of the star' $ poles: These points usually lie hundreds of millions of kilometers from the star; (ar out of its gravity well; even weak gravitational tides can rip JumpShip apart: The jump points remote ocation means that JumpShip passengers and cargo must spend several days aboard DropShip_ traveling between the JumpShip ad its planet of origin or destination; Some JumpShip captains usually those transporting illicit cargos; use pirate jump points These nonstandard points are free from gravitational tides formed by the motion of the sys- tem' planets and moons around its star. Because these pirate points move constantly; only the most skilled navigators attempt t0 use them. JumpShip $ KF drive requires cons derable amount of energy; far more than fusion reactors alone supply:  The drive commonly charged with solar sail; solar energy col: ector often kilometer or more in diameter. Solar sails are the preferred method for recharging JumpShip drives because the energy they collect free_ though occasionally JumpShip cap tains will buy charge from recharge station use thelr Own fusion reactors charge the drive. exists in spite of the Liaos best efforts The state has never adopted formal religion and only interferes directly when reli: gious disputes threaten the state In such cases_ state retalia tion swift and merciless For centuries however, House Liao has worked indirectly to replace formal religious beliets with state approved philosophies specifically the Korvin Doctrine the Sarna Mandate and the Lorix  Order, which   promote the Confederation'$   attempts expand uphold the ultimate authority of the Chancellor and the state and provide the dic tums requiring all citizens to support both of the above_ Among tne government'$ tools for encouraging" these philosophies are philosophical examinations ad courts of inquiry for those deemed hold questionable beliefs_ As long as Capellan citi- zens maintain respect tor the state , however; they may worship as tney see fit. Among the smaller Periphery nations most religions main tain low profile and are tolerant of other faiths. Many Inner Sphere religious scholars travel to the Periphery every year t0 study with its inhabitants; The religious harmony of the Taurian Concordat and the Magistracy of Canopus particular are examples from which the Inner Sphere could learn Despite the Inner Sphere neo-feudal bent; no scion of any Great House claims the divine right rulership that was hallmark of historica feudalism on Terra ancient Terran feu- society. rulers often claimed they were chosen by God and thus had divine right to govern their people as they saw fit: In modern times the only people known to make this claim are occasional cult eaders and minor rulers particularly in  the Periphery states.
